message: Question: What are the latest treatment options for this patient? Patient Case: Michael Anderson is a 64-year-old male from Boston, Massachusetts, USA. He weighs 81 kg and is 178 cm tall (BMI 25.6). He has a significant smoking history of 40 pack-years but quit 5 years ago. His medical history includes hypertension (well controlled), type 2 diabetes (HbA1c ~7.2%), and mild hyperlipidemia. His father died of lung cancer at age 70. He was diagnosed in 2023 with non-small cell lung cancer (NSCLC), adenocarcinoma subtype, at Stage IIIA (locally advanced). Diagnostic imaging revealed a 4.2 cm mass in the right upper lobe with mediastinal lymph node involvement, but no distant metastasis. Brain MRI was negative. Molecular testing showed: EGFR mutation: Negative ALK rearrangement: Negative PD-L1 expression: 60%. Initial treatment included concurrent chemoradiation with carboplatin and paclitaxel alongside 60 Gy radiation over 30 fractions. Following this, he was started on consolidation immunotherapy with durvalumab, administered every 2 weeks for a planned duration of 12 months. As of 2026, he has completed immunotherapy and is in the surveillance phase. His latest CT scans show stable disease with no progression or new lesions. He continues regular follow-up imaging every 3–6 months.
